rationale: The citing paper does more than list Shiv, Carmon, and Ariely (2005) as background; it uses that placebo work as an explicit conceptual contrast and benchmark. In Study 4 and the General Discussion, the authors invoke Shiv et al.'s nonconscious placebo logic (including the salience prediction) to motivate their test and interpret why their own effect appears conscious and self-efficacy driven. This role materially shapes how the citing paper positions its mechanism and contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper refines the prior placebo-based account by arguing that brand-related performance effects can operate through a distinct conscious self-efficacy pathway under its conditions, rather than through the nonconscious placebo route emphasized in Shiv et al. (2005). It uses Shiv et al. as a theoretical benchmark to establish these boundary conditions.
